theme: Slice a sheet into valued pieces, cuts never rejoin
title: Order Slab
seed_problem: guillotine cutting stock: carve a sheet into demanded valued rectangles
mutation: composite: exact-size guillotine cutting stock with per-type sale CAPS, scored by the leaf-size distribution of the WHOLE cut tree (fragments never recombine, no compaction) rather than by the piece a cut immediately yields; the objective is a bounded, cap-saturated value whose optimum needs matching the band-height/width leaf distribution to the demand multiset, not grabbing the highest-value piece.
strategies: trivial: best single shelf -- one band of the type maximizing v*min(c, W/w) (reproduces the checker baseline B, ratio ~0.1)

greedy: value-first shelf packing -- open bands top-down, each filled with copies of the highest PER-PIECE value type that still fits and has cap (grabs shiny needles, one type per band, wastes width, ignores density and height fit)

strong (insight): choose each band's height and its width-mix to maximize value DENSITY under the global caps -- a density-ordered bounded fill per candidate height, committing the best band each step -> diversifies to match the demand leaf-size distribution
beyond strong: true 2D guillotine knapsack / DP over band heights and stacked sub-columns with shared caps -- leaves headroom above the reference
